description_long: This is a robust hammer featuring a solid, rectangular metal head with slightly beveled edges and a flat striking face. The head is made of a dense, gray metallic material, likely steel, and is securely mounted onto a straight, wooden handle. The handle displays a natural wood grain pattern with some visible dark streaks and a squared-off end, suggesting a traditional, utilitarian design. The hammer's head is thick and blocky, with no visible claws or peen, indicating it is likely a sledge or club hammer type, suitable for heavy-duty striking tasks. The overall appearance is functional and sturdy, with no decorative elements or branding visible. The surfaces show some minor discoloration and wear, particularly on the handle, but the tool appears structurally sound and ready for use.
description: A sturdy hammer with a thick, rectangular steel head and a straight wooden handle, showing minor wear. The design is utilitarian and robust, ideal for heavy-duty striking.
description_view_0: Front view shows the squared wooden handle end and the broad, flat face of the metal head.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the full length of the wooden handle and the thickness of the metal head.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the metal head and the way the handle is fitted into it.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the handle's grain and the head's blocky profile.
attribute_handle_type: Straight wooden handle with a squared-off end, visible wood grain, and no additional grip enhancements.
attribute_head_type: Rectangular sledge or club hammer head made of steel, with flat striking faces and beveled edges, no claws or peen.
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: Minor discoloration and wear on the wooden handle, no significant damage or rust visible.
